summary: Deadlines flagged last week are now in effect and have escalated several materials to outdated: the M365 Copilot redesign (Work IQ toggle, Tasks tab) is GA, breaking Day 1 Session 2 decks/videos/labs; Cowork usage-based billing (Copilot Credits, off by default) went live July 1, blocking Day 2 labs without admin setup; and Copilot Studio classic agent creation in Teams retired June 30 with topics removed from the new builder, breaking Day 3 build materials.

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Short framing-level promotional trailer with no specific product UI or pricing detail; unaffected by this week's changes.

materials.title: Manager Kickoff Presentation
materials.finding: Two commercial changes are now in effect that managers must budget for: Cowork usage-based billing (Copilot Credits) went live July 1 with Cowork off by default, and the Copilot Business promo rate ended June 30 (now a permanent SKU at $21/user/month, with Copilot bundled into Business Standard/Premium). The deck likely predates both.
materials.suggestedAction: Add a short slide covering the Cowork Copilot Credits model (off by default, per-task credit consumption, admin spend caps) and the finalized Copilot Business/bundle pricing so managers can set budget expectations. Refresh value framing toward the agent-first language of the 2026 Work Trend Index.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Manager talking points should reflect that Cowork billing and the new Copilot pricing/SKU structure are now live rather than upcoming; guidance framed as 'coming soon' is now dated.
materials.suggestedAction: Update talking points to present Cowork Copilot Credits and Copilot Business pricing as in-effect (July 1), and add a manager FAQ on enabling Cowork and setting spend caps.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Talk track is orientation/framing focused; no product UI walkthrough that this week's changes would invalidate.

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Framing-level trailer; no product specifics affected by recent updates.

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: The 2026 Work Trend Index reframes the AI-first operating model around a 'new agency equation' and four human/agent collaboration modes (Author, Editor, Director, Orchestrator), with agents up ~15x year-over-year. The mindset deck should reflect this agent-first framing and refreshed data.
materials.suggestedAction: Incorporate the four collaboration modes (Author/Editor/Director/Orchestrator) and refresh AI-first framing and stats to the 2026 Work Trend Index.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator narrative for the AI-first mindset can be strengthened with the 2026 Work Trend Index agent-first framing and collaboration-mode vocabulary.
materials.suggestedAction: Add facilitator notes introducing the Author/Editor/Director/Orchestrator collaboration modes when discussing the AI-first operating model.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Talk track references AI-first framing that the 2026 Work Trend Index has updated toward agent orchestration; wording may feel dated.
materials.suggestedAction: Refresh talk-track language to the agent-first 'new agency equation' framing.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 1 — M365 Copilot: From Overwhelmed to In-Command

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Short teaser; no detailed UI walkthrough that the redesign would invalidate.

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: The redesigned Microsoft 365 Copilot app is now GA: a new Tasks tab, a single 'Work IQ' toggle that replaces the old Work/Web toggle, a chat-centered layout, and a pinned-agents flyout. Any screenshots or navigation walkthroughs in the deck showing the previous UI are now inaccurate.
materials.suggestedAction: Recapture all Copilot app screenshots against the new chat-centered UI; replace references to the Work/Web toggle with the Work IQ toggle and cover the new Tasks tab and pinned-agents flyout.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator navigation instructions likely describe the pre-redesign Copilot layout (e.g., Work/Web toggle, old nav), which participants will no longer see.
materials.suggestedAction: Update step-by-step navigation references to match the new Work IQ toggle, Tasks tab, and agent flyout.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: The recorded talk track demonstrates the previous Copilot interface (Work/Web toggle, prior navigation) that has been replaced by the GA redesign, so on-screen demo no longer matches the live product.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record or annotate the demo segments to reflect the new chat-centered UI, Work IQ toggle, and Tasks tab.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: The lab has participants experiment in the M365 Copilot app, but the GA redesign changed the entry points and toggles (Work IQ replacing Work/Web, new Tasks tab, chat-centered layout). Click-path steps referencing the old UI will not match what participants see.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite lab click-paths against the new Copilot UI (Work IQ toggle, Tasks tab, updated navigation) and refresh any embedded screenshots.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 2 — Copilot Cowork: Delegate, Don't Dictate

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Framing-level teaser for Cowork; does not depend on the billing/preview status that changed.

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: Cowork is now GA and, as of July 1, requires usage-based billing (Copilot Credits) and is OFF BY DEFAULT — each task consumes credits based on model, context, tools, and runtime. A deck produced during the free Frontier preview misrepresents both availability and cost.
materials.suggestedAction: Add/replace slides to explain the Copilot Credits usage-based model, that Cowork is off by default and must be enabled by an admin, and set expectations that tasks consume credits. Remove any 'free preview' framing.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator delivery is impacted: unless the tenant admin has enabled Cowork and configured usage-based billing, participants cannot use Cowork at all as of July 1. The guide likely assumes always-on preview access.
materials.suggestedAction: Add a pre-session prerequisite that admins enable Cowork and usage-based billing, plus a fallback plan if credits/billing are not provisioned; update talking points to the credits model.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Talk track may present Cowork as freely available; it should acknowledge the credits-based, off-by-default model now in effect.
materials.suggestedAction: Add a note (or re-record) clarifying Cowork now runs on Copilot Credits and must be enabled.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: This Cowork hands-on lab now depends on the tenant having Cowork enabled and usage-based billing configured (off by default since July 1), and each lab task will consume Copilot Credits. Without admin setup participants are blocked, and there is no cost caveat.
materials.suggestedAction: Add explicit prerequisites (admin-enabled Cowork + billing/credits), warn that lab tasks consume credits, and provide a no-billing fallback exercise.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 3 — Build Your Own Copilot: No Code Required

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Conceptual teaser; not tied to the specific builder UI that changed.

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: Copilot Studio changed materially: classic agent creation from within Teams retired June 30 (web app only now), and the new agent builder is natural-language-first with topics removed and a unified Build/Preview/Evaluate/Monitor layout — with no migration path from classic. Screenshots and step flows built on the classic topic-tree experience are outdated.
materials.suggestedAction: Rebuild the walkthrough on the new Copilot Studio agent builder (natural-language authoring, skills/tools/workflows instead of topics, Build/Preview/Evaluate/Monitor tabs) and remove the Teams-based classic creation path.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator steps that instruct creating a classic agent in Teams or building topic flows no longer work — Teams classic creation is retired and topics are gone from the new builder.
materials.suggestedAction: Revise facilitator steps to the web-based new agent builder and its natural-language/skills model; drop topic-tree and Teams-creation instructions.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: The demo walks through the classic Copilot Studio builder (topics, Teams creation), which has been retired/replaced, so the on-screen product no longer matches.
materials.suggestedAction: Re-record the build demo using the new agent builder experience.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: The lab has participants build an agent in Copilot Studio, but classic agent creation in Teams retired June 30 and the new builder removes topics in favor of natural-language authoring, skills, and workflows. Existing lab steps will not match the current product and cannot be completed as written.
materials.suggestedAction: Rewrite the lab against the new agent builder (natural-language setup, skills/tools/workflows, web app) and remove classic topic/Teams-creation steps.

sessions.sessionTitle: Day 4 — Work Redesign: Rethinking the Work Itself — Not Just Making It Faster

materials.title: Teaser Trailer
materials.finding: Conceptual teaser; not affected by product changes.

materials.title: Presentation
materials.finding: The 2026 Work Trend Index strongly reinforces this session's thesis (Frontier Firms reinvent work rather than bolt AI on; organizational readiness outweighs individual effort ~67% vs 32%). The deck can be refreshed with this framing and current data but is not factually broken.
materials.suggestedAction: Fold in the 2026 Work Trend Index 'Frontier Firm' reinvention framing and refreshed stats to reinforce the work-redesign argument.

materials.title: Facilitator Guide
materials.finding: Facilitator talking points can cite the latest Work Trend Index evidence on reinventing work vs. accelerating existing tasks.
materials.suggestedAction: Add current Work Trend Index talking points on organizational readiness and work reinvention.

materials.title: Video Talk Track Prep
materials.finding: Conceptual talk track on work redesign; no product UI or data that recent changes invalidate.

materials.title: Lab Practice Guide
materials.finding: Work-redesign exercise is conceptual (rethinking workflows) and not tied to any specific tool UI or feature that changed this week.
